name: SkillsBench
full_name: Benchmarking How Well Agent Skills Work Across Diverse Tasks
key_findings: Curated Skills raise avg pass rate by +16.2pp
Self-generated Skills provide negligible or negative benefit
Healthcare benefits most; Software Eng. benefits least
First peer-reviewed benchmark for agent skill evaluation

name: SE-Bench
key_findings: Obfuscated API prevents memorization cheating
Closed-book paradigm forces true knowledge internalization
Reveals gap between context-use and genuine learning
Rigorous diagnostic platform for self-evolution

name: StuLife
full_name: Building Self-Evolving Agents via Experience-Driven Lifelong Learning
key_findings: Simulates student's full college journey across 3 phases, 10 scenarios
4 paradigm shifts: Simulation→Reality, Passive→Proactive, Context→Memory, Imitation→Learning
Introduces ELL (Experience-driven Lifelong Learning) framework
Evaluates long-horizon experience-driven growth

name: AgentGym
full_name: Evolving Large Language Model-based Agents across Diverse Environments
key_findings: 14 environment types, 89 task types
AgentEvol enables self-evolution without human supervision
Agents generalize skills to unseen tasks after evolution
Unified HTTP-based architecture for standardized evaluation

name: TRACE
full_name: Towards Self-Evolving Benchmarks: Synthesizing Agent Trajectories via Test-Time Exploration
key_findings: Benchmark Complexity Evolution (TRACE) framework
Agents explore and evolve tasks to higher difficulty
Validate-by-Reproduce paradigm ensures trajectory validity
Paradigm shift from static to dynamic self-evolving benchmarks

name: CASCADE / SciSkillBench
full_name: Cumulative Agentic Skill Creation through Autonomous Development and Evolution
key_findings: 93.3% success rate with GPT-5 vs 35.4% without evolution (+57.9pp)
Cumulative skill building: each skill builds on prior ones
SciSkillBench: 116 science research tasks
Autonomous skill development and evolution loop

name: Evo-Memory
full_name: Benchmarking LLM Agent Test-time Learning with Self-Evolving Memory
key_findings: Streaming benchmark for self-evolving memory evaluation
Sequential task streams test memory update and retention
Evaluates test-time learning without retraining
Comprehensive framework for memory evolution

name: AgentSkillOS
full_name: Organizing, Orchestrating, and Benchmarking Agent Skills at Ecosystem Scale
key_findings: AgentSkillOS outperforms native skill provisioning at scale
Tree-based retrieval approximates oracle skill selection
Performance advantage persists as skill ecosystem scales
Addresses skill organization at ecosystem level

name: SWE-Bench Pro
full_name: SWE-Bench Pro: Can AI Agents Solve Long-Horizon Software Engineering Tasks?
key_findings: 1,865 human-verified tasks from 41 actively maintained repos
Contamination-resistant design
Long-horizon tasks requiring hours-to-days for human engineers
Multi-file patches across enterprise codebases

name: Multi-SWE-Bench
full_name: Multi-SWE-bench: A Multilingual Benchmark for Issue Resolving
key_findings: 2,132 instances curated by 68 expert annotators
First multilingual code issue benchmark (8 languages)
Covers Java, TypeScript, JS, Go, Rust, C, C++
Accompanied by Multi-SWE-RL reinforcement learning dataset

name: Vibe Code Bench
full_name: Vibe Code Bench: Evaluating AI Models on End-to-End Web Application Development
key_findings: 100 web app specs (50 public + 50 held-out)
964 browser-based workflows, 10,131 substeps
End-to-end evaluation of vibe coding agents
GPT-5.4 leads at 67.42% overall accuracy

name: MLE-Bench Lite
full_name: MLE-bench: Evaluating Machine Learning Agents on Machine Learning Engineering
key_findings: Lite version: 18 tasks, balanced across modalities and difficulty
Full version: 75 Kaggle ML competitions
Tests complete ML workflows: data prep, training, submission
Best agents achieve bronze medal performance on some competitions

name: GDPval-AA
full_name: GDPval: Evaluating AI Model Performance on Real-World Economically Valuable Tasks
key_findings: 1,320 tasks across 44 occupations and 9 industries
Tasks designed by professionals with 14+ years experience
GDPval-AA is Artificial Analysis' primary agentic performance metric
Tests knowledge work: presentations, data analysis, video editing

name: Toolathlon
full_name: The Tool Decathlon: Benchmarking Language Agents for Diverse, Realistic, and Long-Horizon Task Execution
key_findings: 108 tasks across 32 real software applications, 604 tools
~20 interaction turns per task on average
Covers everyday to enterprise platforms
Strictly verifiable through dedicated evaluation scripts

name: MM-ClawBench
full_name: Benchmarking Hierarchical Capabilities of Multimodal Agents in Professional Software Environments
key_findings: 436 realistic work and research tasks
First capability hierarchy for professional software agent use
Spans 6 disciplines and 13 core professional software tools
Tests hierarchical multimodal agent capabilities
